questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The curve $x y=c(c\gt0)$ and the circle $x^2+y^2=1$ touch at two points, then distance between the points of contacts is
questions.solution:
The curve $x y=c$ and the circle $x^2+y^2=1$ touches each other so
$x^2+\frac{c^2}{x^2}-1=0 \Rightarrow x^4-x^2+c^2=0$
will have equal roots so $(-1)^2-4 \mathrm{c}^2=0 \Rightarrow 4 \mathrm{c}^2=1$
$\Rightarrow c^2=\frac{1}{4} \Rightarrow c=\frac{1}{2}$
Roots of the equations $x^4-x^2+\frac{1}{4}=0$ are $x= \pm \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{y}=\mp \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} .$
Clearly distance between the points of contact $=2$ units
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $p$ and $q$ be the longest and the shortest distances respectively of the point $(-7,2)$ from any point $(\alpha, \beta)$ on the curve whose equation is $x^2+y^2-10 x-14 y-51=0$ then G.M of $p$ and $q$ is
questions.solution: The centre c of the circle $=(5,7)$ and the radius $\begin{aligned}& =\sqrt{5^2+7^2+51}=5 \sqrt{5} \\& P C=\sqrt{12^2+5^2}=13\end{aligned}$ $\Rightarrow q=P A=13-5 \sqrt{5}$ and $p=P B=13+5 \sqrt{3}$ $\therefore$ G.M of $p$ and $q=\sqrt{p q}=\sqrt{(13-5 \sqrt{5})(13+5 \sqrt{5})}$ $=\sqrt{169-125}=2 \sqrt{11}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
If a variable line $L: 3 x-2 y-4+\lambda(x-2 y+4)=0$ where $\lambda$ is a parameter is passing through a fixed point $P(a, b)$ and $S: x^2+y^2=8$ is a circle, then:
\(\begin{array}{|c|l|c|c|} \hline & \text{List-I} & & \text{List-II} \\ \hline(P) & (a+b) \text{ is equal to} & (1) & 2 \sqrt{2} \\ \hline(Q) & \text{Length of the tangent from 'P' to the circle 'S' is} & (2) & 4 \sqrt{2} \\ \hline(\mathrm{R}) & \text{Least distance of 'P' to the circle 'S' is} & (3) & 6 \sqrt{2} \\ \hline(\mathrm{S}) & \begin{array}{l}  \text{Least radius of the circle whose centre is 'P' and it contains the} \\ \text{circle 'S', is} \end{array} & (4) & 2 \sqrt{6} \\ \hline & & (5) & 8 \\ \hline \end{array}\)
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{L}: 3 x-2 y-4+\lambda(x-2 y+4)=0 \\
& P(a, b) \equiv(4,4) \\
& \mathrm{S}: x^2+y^2=8
\end{aligned}$
(P) $a+b=8$
(Q) $L_T=\sqrt{S_1}=\sqrt{16+16-8}=2 \sqrt{6}$
(R) Least distance $=O P-r=4 \sqrt{2}-2 \sqrt{2}=2 \sqrt{2}$
(S) Least radius of the circle containing the given circle is
$=O P+r=4 \sqrt{2}+2 \sqrt{2}=6 \sqrt{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
